notes: Default gravity deck for the Lovers apartment. Small intimate beats (Reed crosses the room, the record changes side) cut with pressure beats (the friend knocks awkwardly, the landlord rings the buzzer). The diner sounds come up through the floor under every card.

deck.title: reed crosses the room
deck.flavor: reed has moved to a new corner. you didn't see them get up. you can hear them now — they are at the easel, looking at the painting.

deck.title: the record changes side
deck.flavor: the record finished its side. somebody — you, maybe — flipped it. you don't remember flipping it. the b-side is the one you used to skip; tonight you're letting it play.

deck.title: a box opens
deck.flavor: the tape on a box gave up. the contents are visible — a sweater that's reed's, a paperback that's yours, the small ceramic dish you brought back from somewhere.

deck.title: the radiator clicks louder
deck.flavor: the radiator is doing the thing it does at three pm. the apartment has been over-warm since october. you and reed used to argue about the thermostat. tonight you don't.

deck.title: the window breeze
deck.flavor: january air through the cracked window. cold and clean. you can smell the diner's fryer through it for a moment, then the wind shifts and you can't.

deck.title: reed holds a thing
deck.flavor: reed is holding something and not putting it in a box. they hold it for a long minute. they put it back where it was, not in the box.

deck.title: the diner's call bell
deck.flavor: the bell two floors below. john is in the weeds. you can hear the rush starting from up here. the floor vibrates a tiny amount when somebody downstairs runs.

deck.title: the painting glances at you
deck.flavor: the unfinished painting on the easel is doing something. you can't say it's looking at you because it doesn't have eyes. it's looking at you.

deck.title: an awkward silence
deck.flavor: neither of you is speaking. the silence has shape. it is a person-shaped silence and it is sitting in the third corner of the bed.

deck.title: the kettle finds itself
deck.flavor: the kettle clicks. somebody put it on. you both look at it. neither of you remembers turning the burner.

deck.title: reed steps on a creaky board
deck.flavor: the floor announces them. the same board that has announced everyone who has crossed this apartment, including the two of you, every day for a year.

deck.title: the phone in the diner rings
deck.flavor: you can hear it through the floor. somebody is calling from somewhere that has a phone. john answers. john always answers.

deck.title: reed speaks
deck.flavor: they say a thing they have not said before. the sentence has been with them for a while. you can hear that. the room holds it after they're done.

deck.title: the painting is done
deck.flavor: the painting on the easel is finished. you don't remember finishing it. reed doesn't remember either. it just got done while you were both standing nearby.

deck.title: the afternoon holds
deck.flavor: the room is steady. you both notice it at the same moment. neither of you says it. the noticing is enough.
